title: Fix the Simple Array Range Finder

statement.markdown: Write a function to find the range of a given array of integers. The range is defined as the difference between the maximum and minimum values in the array. If the array is empty, the function should return -1.
statement.inputFormat: The function will be called with an array of integers as its only argument.
statement.outputFormat: The function should return the range of the array, or -1 if the array is empty.
statement.notes: You can assume that the input array will only contain integers.

constraints.description: The length of the input array

examples.explanation: The range of the array [1, 2, 3, 4, 5] is 5 - 1 = 4
The array is empty, so the function returns -1
The range of the array [-5, 2, 8, 7, 3, 1] is 8 - (-5) = 13

hints.text: Check the length of the input array before trying to find the range

editorial.title: Fix the Simple Array Range Finder
editorial.intuition: To find the range of an array, you need to find the maximum and minimum values in the array.
editorial.bruteForce: You can use a simple loop to find the maximum and minimum values in the array.
editorial.optimalSolution: You can use the built-in Math.max and Math.min functions to find the maximum and minimum values in the array.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to check if the array is empty
Not handling the case where the array contains only one element
